cards.title: Panchakarma — Principles & Philosophy

alerts.label: Panchakarma — The Five Therapeutic Actions
alerts.text: Panchakarma (pañcakarma) is Ayurveda's primary detoxification and purification system. The term means "five actions" — five main therapeutic procedures that eliminate accumulated toxins (ama) and vitiated doshas from specific body channels (srotas). Panchakarma is always preceded by purvakarma (preparatory procedures: snehana/oleation and swedana/sudation) and followed by paschatkarma (post-treatment rehabilitation). It is a complete therapeutic programme, not individual treatments.

rows: Asthma, chronic cough, sinusitis, skin diseases (kapha), obesity, psoriasis
Skin diseases (pitta), liver disorders, NAFLD, inflammatory bowel, jaundice, migraine, herpes
Constipation, neurological disorders, musculoskeletal, IBS, reproductive disorders. Most important of the 5 karmas — addresses vata which controls all movement.
Sinusitis, migraine, CNS disorders, cognitive decline, cervical spondylosis, facial palsy, nasal polyps
Skin diseases (eczema, psoriasis), inflammatory arthritis, gout, varicose veins, local infections. Leech therapy most common modern application.

cards._legacyHtml:
Panchakarma — The Five Therapeutic Actions
Panchakarma (pañcakarma) is Ayurveda's primary detoxification and purification system. The term means "five actions" — five main therapeutic procedures that eliminate accumulated toxins (ama) and vitiated doshas from specific body channels (srotas). Panchakarma is always preceded by purvakarma (preparatory procedures: snehana/oleation and swedana/sudation) and followed by paschatkarma (post-treatment rehabilitation). It is a complete therapeutic programme, not individual treatments.
KarmaSanskritRoutePrimary Dosha AddressedMain Indication
VamanaTherapeutic vomitingOral (emetic decoctions)Kapha (from stomach/lungs)Asthma, chronic cough, sinusitis, skin diseases (kapha), obesity, psoriasis
VirechanaTherapeutic purgationOral (purgatives)Pitta (from small intestine/liver)Skin diseases (pitta), liver disorders, NAFLD, inflammatory bowel, jaundice, migraine, herpes
BastiTherapeutic enemaRectal (herbal decoctions or medicated oils)Vata (from colon — vata's seat)Constipation, neurological disorders, musculoskeletal, IBS, reproductive disorders. Most important of the 5 karmas — addresses vata which controls all movement.
NasyaNasal administrationNasal (medicated oils, powders)Kapha above clavicle (head and neck)Sinusitis, migraine, CNS disorders, cognitive decline, cervical spondylosis, facial palsy, nasal polyps
RaktamokshanaBlood purification/lettingVarious (leech therapy, venesection, cupping)Rakta (blood — pitta-vitiated)Skin diseases (eczema, psoriasis), inflammatory arthritis, gout, varicose veins, local infections. Leech therapy most common modern application.

cards.title: Purvakarma — Preparatory Procedures

alerts.label: Purvakarma is MANDATORY Before Any Main Panchakarma Procedure
alerts.text: Purvakarma (preparatory procedures) mobilises ama and vitiated doshas from peripheral tissues to the GI tract (the site of elimination). Performing main panchakarma without purvakarma is like trying to sweep a room without loosening the dust first — ineffective and potentially harmful.

steps.title: Snehana (Oleation) — Internal & External
steps.text: Internal snehana (antah snehana): medicated ghee (ghrita) administered orally in increasing doses over 3–7 days (typically 30ml → 60ml → 90ml → 120ml). Softens and lubricates body channels. Signs of proper internal oleation: oiliness of stool, absence of hunger (oleation suppresses agni), skin lustre. External snehana: Abhyanga — full body warm medicated oil massage (60–90 min). Specific oils: Dhanvantaram (vata), Mahanarayan (musculoskeletal), Ksheerabala (neurological). Post-massage steam (see swedana below).

steps.title: Swedana (Sudation/Steam Therapy)
steps.text: Immediately after external snehana. Types: Bashpa sweda (steam bath in steam cabinet — full body). Nadi sweda (steam pipe directed at specific joints). Pinda sweda (bolus massage — see below). Duration: 10–20 min until profuse sweating. Endpoints: skin becomes soft and moist, ama is liquefied and mobilised, patient feels light. Post-swedana: patient must be kept warm and avoid cold — open channels are vulnerable.

steps.title: Pinda Sweda — Special Bolus Therapies
steps.text: Njavara Kizhi: rice (njavara variety) cooked in milk and herbal decoction, formed into boluses in cloth, massaged over body at 45°C. Indication: neurological conditions, post-stroke rehabilitation, muscular atrophy, Parkinson's. Elakizhi: fresh medicinal leaves (nirgundi, vitex, calotropis, etc.) filled in cloth bolus. Indication: musculoskeletal pain, arthritis, spondylosis. Chakra Basti: medicated oil retained in dough well placed over specific joints or spine (see previous reference).

Purvakarma is MANDATORY Before Any Main Panchakarma Procedure
Purvakarma (preparatory procedures) mobilises ama and vitiated doshas from peripheral tissues to the GI tract (the site of elimination). Performing main panchakarma without purvakarma is like trying to sweep a room without loosening the dust first — ineffective and potentially harmful.

Snehana (Oleation) — Internal & External Internal snehana (antah snehana): medicated ghee (ghrita) administered orally in increasing doses over 3–7 days (typically 30ml → 60ml → 90ml → 120ml). Softens and lubricates body channels. Signs of proper internal oleation: oiliness of stool, absence of hunger (oleation suppresses agni), skin lustre. External snehana: Abhyanga — full body warm medicated oil massage (60–90 min). Specific oils: Dhanvantaram (vata), Mahanarayan (musculoskeletal), Ksheerabala (neurological). Post-massage steam (see swedana below).

Swedana (Sudation/Steam Therapy) Immediately after external snehana. Types: Bashpa sweda (steam bath in steam cabinet — full body). Nadi sweda (steam pipe directed at specific joints). Pinda sweda (bolus massage — see below). Duration: 10–20 min until profuse sweating. Endpoints: skin becomes soft and moist, ama is liquefied and mobilised, patient feels light. Post-swedana: patient must be kept warm and avoid cold — open channels are vulnerable.
3
Pinda Sweda — Special Bolus Therapies Njavara Kizhi: rice (njavara variety) cooked in milk and herbal decoction, formed into boluses in cloth, massaged over body at 45°C. Indication: neurological conditions, post-stroke rehabilitation, muscular atrophy, Parkinson's. Elakizhi: fresh medicinal leaves (nirgundi, vitex, calotropis, etc.) filled in cloth bolus. Indication: musculoskeletal pain, arthritis, spondylosis. Chakra Basti: medicated oil retained in dough well placed over specific joints or spine (see previous reference).

cards.title: Special Panchakarma Treatments — Shirodhara & Shirobasti

alerts.label: Shirodhara — The Most Recognisable Panchakarma Procedure
alerts.text: Shirodhara (shiro = head, dhara = continuous stream) involves a continuous flow of warm medicated liquid (oil, milk, buttermilk, decoctions) onto the forehead (third eye — ajna chakra region) in a slow oscillating stream for 30–60 minutes. It induces profound mental relaxation, normalises HPA axis, and is neurologically restorative. Multiple RCTs confirm efficacy for insomnia, anxiety, hypertension, and PTSD.

rows: Medicated sesame oil (Dhanvantaram, Ksheerabala, Brahmi oil)
Vata disorders, neurological conditions, insomnia, anxiety, cognitive decline, facial palsy
Pitta disorders, burning sensations, migraines, hypertension, menopausal symptoms, hair loss
Pitta + vata, psoriasis of scalp, stress, premature greying, burning sensations of scalp
Kapha-vata conditions, musculoskeletal, chronic inflammatory conditions

pearls.label: 🟡 Shirodhara Evidence
pearls.text: RCTs for Shirodhara: significant reduction in insomnia (ISI score), anxiety (HAM-A), hypertension, and PTSD symptoms. Proposed mechanisms: direct activation of forehead mechanoreceptors → vagal afferents → hypothalamic modulation → cortisol ↓, melatonin ↑. EEG during shirodhara: alpha and theta wave dominance (relaxation/meditative state). fMRI: increased prefrontal cortex activity (executive control) + decreased amygdala activation (anxiety reduction). This is one of the best-evidenced Ayurvedic procedures.

steps.title: Shirodhara Protocol
steps.text: Patient position: supine, eyes covered. Oil heated to 37–42°C (body temperature — never hot). Dhara vessel suspended above forehead. Oil pours in oscillating stream from hairline to hairline (not onto face, not into eyes or ears). Oscillation rate: slow and rhythmic. Therapist must maintain constant stream temperature and flow. Post-procedure: DO NOT allow patient to sit up rapidly (orthostatic hypotension). Rest 20–30 min. Head wrapped to retain heat. Avoid cold, wind, and mental stress post-treatment.

cards._legacyHtml:
Shirodhara — The Most Recognisable Panchakarma Procedure
Shirodhara (shiro = head, dhara = continuous stream) involves a continuous flow of warm medicated liquid (oil, milk, buttermilk, decoctions) onto the forehead (third eye — ajna chakra region) in a slow oscillating stream for 30–60 minutes. It induces profound mental relaxation, normalises HPA axis, and is neurologically restorative. Multiple RCTs confirm efficacy for insomnia, anxiety, hypertension, and PTSD.
Shirodhara TypeFluid UsedIndicationDuration
TailadharaMedicated sesame oil (Dhanvantaram, Ksheerabala, Brahmi oil)Vata disorders, neurological conditions, insomnia, anxiety, cognitive decline, facial palsy45–60 min, 7–14 days
KsheeradharaMedicated milk (with Brahmi, ashwagandha decoctions)Pitta disorders, burning sensations, migraines, hypertension, menopausal symptoms, hair loss30–45 min, 7–14 days
TakradharaMedicated buttermilk (with amalaki, sandalwood)Pitta + vata, psoriasis of scalp, stress, premature greying, burning sensations of scalp30–45 min, 7–21 days
KwathadharaHerbal decoctions (various)Kapha-vata conditions, musculoskeletal, chronic inflammatory conditions30 min, 7 days
S
Shirodhara Protocol Patient position: supine, eyes covered. Oil heated to 37–42°C (body temperature — never hot). Dhara vessel suspended above forehead. Oil pours in oscillating stream from hairline to hairline (not onto face, not into eyes or ears). Oscillation rate: slow and rhythmic. Therapist must maintain constant stream temperature and flow. Post-procedure: DO NOT allow patient to sit up rapidly (orthostatic hypotension). Rest 20–30 min. Head wrapped to retain heat. Avoid cold, wind, and mental stress post-treatment.
🟡 Shirodhara Evidence
RCTs for Shirodhara: significant reduction in insomnia (ISI score), anxiety (HAM-A), hypertension, and PTSD symptoms. Proposed mechanisms: direct activation of forehead mechanoreceptors → vagal afferents → hypothalamic modulation → cortisol ↓, melatonin ↑. EEG during shirodhara: alpha and theta wave dominance (relaxation/meditative state). fMRI: increased prefrontal cortex activity (executive control) + decreased amygdala activation (anxiety reduction). This is one of the best-evidenced Ayurvedic procedures.

cards.title: Leech Therapy (Jalaukavacharana) — Clinical Protocol

alerts.label: Medicinal Leech Therapy — Modern Evidence
alerts.text: Medicinal leeches (Hirudo medicinalis) produce hirudin (anticoagulant), hyaluronidase (spreading factor), destabilase (clot lysis), and anaesthetic compounds in their saliva. Applications: venous congestion (microsurgery flap salvage — Grade A evidence), osteoarthritis, varicose veins, chronic skin conditions.

rows: Localised venous congestion post-surgery ★★★, OA of knee ★★ (RCT evidence), varicose veins, haematoma, skin diseases (psoriasis, eczema — Ayurvedic), thrombophlebitis, gout (localised)

Bleeding disorders (haemophilia, anticoagulant therapy), anaemia (<10g/dL Hb), pregnancy, allergy to leech antigens (test first), HIV/Hepatitis (leech cannot be reused — strict infection control), immunosuppression, malignancy at site

1) Starved leech (30 min to 2 days). 2) Clean site with water (no alcohol — repels leeches). 3) Apply leech — it attaches in 1–5 min (may need a small nick with lancet). 4) Leech feeds 20–60 min, detaches when full. 5) NEVER forcibly remove (injects bacteria). 6) Post-removal bleeding: 30 min to 6 hours (hirudin effect). Cover with absorbent dressing. 7) Dispose of leech in 70% alcohol — NEVER reuse (infection risk).

Monitor for: excessive bleeding (rare — apply firm pressure if >200ml total loss), allergic reaction (urticaria at site — antihistamine), secondary infection (7–10 days post — Aeromonas hydrophila from leech gut — may need prophylactic antibiotic). Site heals in 1–2 weeks.

Medicinal Leech Therapy — Modern Evidence
Medicinal leeches (Hirudo medicinalis) produce hirudin (anticoagulant), hyaluronidase (spreading factor), destabilase (clot lysis), and anaesthetic compounds in their saliva. Applications: venous congestion (microsurgery flap salvage — Grade A evidence), osteoarthritis, varicose veins, chronic skin conditions.
ParameterDetails
IndicationsLocalised venous congestion post-surgery ★★★, OA of knee ★★ (RCT evidence), varicose veins, haematoma, skin diseases (psoriasis, eczema — Ayurvedic), thrombophlebitis, gout (localised)
ContraindicationsBleeding disorders (haemophilia, anticoagulant therapy), anaemia (<10g/dL Hb), pregnancy, allergy to leech antigens (test first), HIV/Hepatitis (leech cannot be reused — strict infection control), immunosuppression, malignancy at site
Procedure1) Starved leech (30 min to 2 days). 2) Clean site with water (no alcohol — repels leeches). 3) Apply leech — it attaches in 1–5 min (may need a small nick with lancet). 4) Leech feeds 20–60 min, detaches when full. 5) NEVER forcibly remove (injects bacteria). 6) Post-removal bleeding: 30 min to 6 hours (hirudin effect). Cover with absorbent dressing. 7) Dispose of leech in 70% alcohol — NEVER reuse (infection risk).
Post-procedure careMonitor for: excessive bleeding (rare — apply firm pressure if >200ml total loss), allergic reaction (urticaria at site — antihistamine), secondary infection (7–10 days post — Aeromonas hydrophila from leech gut — may need prophylactic antibiotic). Site heals in 1–2 weeks.
